definitions.definition: A double-stranded helix-shaped molecule containing genetic information in the form of nucleotides. It is the carrier of hereditary traits from one generation to the next.
A single-stranded molecule containing genetic information in the form of nucleotides. It plays a crucial role in protein synthesis and the transmission of genetic information.
The basic structural unit of DNA and RNA, composed of a sugar molecule (deoxyribose in DNA and ribose in RNA), a phosphate group, and a nitrogenous base.
One of the five carbon-containing compounds found in DNA and RNA: adenine, thymine, cytosine, guanine, and uracil.
A nitrogenous base found in DNA and RNA, which pairs with thymine in DNA and uracil in RNA.
A nitrogenous base found in DNA, which pairs with adenine.
A nitrogenous base found in DNA and RNA, which pairs with guanine.
A nitrogenous base found in DNA and RNA, which pairs with cytosine.
A nitrogenous base found in RNA, which pairs with adenine.
The twisted, ladder-like structure of DNA, formed by the pairing of complementary nitrogenous bases.
The backbone of DNA, composed of sugar molecules (deoxyribose) and phosphate groups.
The process by which nitrogenous bases in DNA or RNA pair with each other to form a complementary strand.
The pairing of nitrogenous bases in DNA or RNA, where adenine pairs with thymine (in DNA) or uracil (in RNA), and cytosine pairs with guanine.
The process by which a DNA molecule is duplicated to produce two identical copies.
The process by which genetic information in DNA is copied into a complementary RNA molecule.
The process by which the sequence of nucleotides in an RNA molecule is decoded to produce a polypeptide chain.
A unit of heredity that carries information from one generation to the next, typically composed of a sequence of nucleotides in DNA.
A thread-like structure composed of DNA and proteins, found in the nucleus of eukaryotic cells.
A variant of a gene, which occupies a specific locus on a chromosome.
A specific position on a chromosome where a gene or other genetic element is located.
